publicSideBand: The city covers utility-owned work at no direct charge as part of the five-year replacement program
privateSideBand: Most invited homeowners can have lead service lines replaced free of charge through the current Racine program
fullReplacementBand: Full replacement is described as free of charge for most invited cases under the annual program
housingTypeAssumption: Racine publishes program-funded replacement language rather than contractor bid amounts
permitAssumption: Eligibility depends on dashboard records annual notices and city invitation rules
restorationAssumption: The city and contractors manage restoration through the replacement program
methodologySummary: Racine ties cost to annual invitation-based replacement batches inside its multi-year city program rather than to an open homeowner quote model and says most invited homes can be completed for free.
ownerTriggerSummary: Owners avoid direct cost only when Racine identifies the address through its records and invitation workflow and moves the property into the current city-managed replacement batch.
